Spatial Anomaly Detected.
Source: network_tap
Vector: <|spatial_vector|>
Network Tap — HTTPS Beacon Activity.
Source: 10.9.231.246 → 104.21.215.196:4443
  beacon_count=233  interval_s=128
  inter_arrival_cv=0.1145  jitter_pct_observed=11
  http_method=GET  uri=/telemetry/push
  payload_size_bytes=347 (consistent)
  cert_self_signed=True  cert_valid_days=53
  dst_asn=AS209 CenturyLink  JA3=044d24dc
  port_class=c2-like  is_internal_dst=False
  observation_window_first_seen=01:23

<analysis>
[AXIS 1] Benign Alternative Assessment:
  Monitoring agents and CDN health checks produce regular outbound HTTPS — but they target registered vendor domains with CA-signed certificates, not commodity VPS infrastructure. Real health checks have bounded session counts and business-hours activity.
[AXIS 2] Behavioral Proof Assessment:
  inter_arrival_cv=0.1145 (near zero = machine-generated, not human-driven). beacon_count=233 identical GET requests to /telemetry/push at 128s ± 11% = automated beacon loop. cert_self_signed=True, valid_days=53 (attacker-generated infra). dst=AS209 CenturyLink (commodity VPS, not CDN or enterprise infrastructure). payload_size consistent (347B) = structured protocol, not user data. First seen 01:23 (off-hours = no user session active).
[AXIS 3] Entity Coverage:
  Host 10.9.231.246 is beaconing to C2 infrastructure at 104.21.215.196:4443. At 128s interval, attacker receives callback every session. Beacon survives sleep/shutdown via reconnect logic.
[CONCLUSION] HTTPS C2 beacon confirmed — machine interval + VPS destination + consistent payload size.
</analysis>
TRUE POSITIVE. MITRE T1071.001 (Web Protocols) + T1573.001 (Encrypted Channel: Symmetric). Block destination IP, isolate source host, capture beacon pcap.
RECOMMENDED_ACTION: contain
